title: Swarm invariants test (lock/lease + cancellation + loop-counter + ledger consistency)
goal: Per spec §6.7.5 (AC-SWARM-HARNESS-INVARIANTS) the harness must surface lock/lease behavior under contention, cancellation propagation when sessions terminate mid-mutation, loop-counter behavior (HBR-SWARM-002) when a session loops on a stuck precondition, and ProcessOwnershipLedger consistency when multiple sessions write concurrently. Failures here gate KERNEL-004 acceptance.
expected_diff_shape: New test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/swarm_invariants_tests.rs exercising the four invariants; HBR_SWARM_INVARIANT_FAIL typed receipt for any violation; gov-check sub-check.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.7.5 four invariants must be tested as wire conditions, not unit-tested in isolation. (1) Lock/lease contention: 16 sessions race on a single resource lease; assert lease is granted to all 16 in some serializable order; assert no lease is granted to more than one session at a time (use a shared atomic that increments on grant and decrements on release, asserts max value == 1). (2) Cancellation propagation: 8 sessions start a long-running mutation; trigger cancellation after 100ms; assert all 8 cancellations propagate within bounded time (default 500ms upper bound) by polling each session's status — fails if any session is still running at deadline. (3) Loop-counter (HBR-SWARM-002): a session deliberately loops on a stuck precondition (e.g., waits for an event that never fires); the HBR-SWARM-002 loop counter must fire and emit FR-EVT-LOOP-CAP receipt after the configured cap (default 1000 iterations) — assert receipt emitted and session terminated. (4) ProcessLedger consistency: 8 sessions each spawn 10 processes; assert every START has a matching STOP after the swarm completes; assert no duplicate process_uuid across sessions; assert ledger rows correlate session_id correctly. Any invariant failure emits an HBR_SWARM_INVARIANT_FAIL typed receipt (canonical JSON per MT-006 pattern). Justfile recipe `hbr-swarm-invariants` wired into gov-check umbrella as the canonical AC-SWARM-HARNESS-INVARIANTS evidence.
risk_if_missed: Without invariant tests, AC-SWARM-HARNESS-INVARIANTS has no evidence; concurrency regressions in any of the four areas ship unobserved.
validator_focus: Each invariant has its own assertion (not aggregated); failing receipt is structured (machine-readable); loop-counter test uses the actual HBR-SWARM-002 cap from MT-008 reclaim infrastructure (not a duplicated counter).

minimum_controls: Each of the four invariants asserts independently (no aggregated pass).
Cancellation deadline is enforced via timeout (not best-effort).
ProcessLedger consistency check asserts no duplicate process_uuid AND correct session correlation.
